This question asks if the US will accuse Russia of invading any country other than Ukraine by July 1, 2025 (a window of just 2 months from the knowledge cutoff of May 1, 2025). The base rate for new Russian invasions is already low, and Russia's military remains deeply committed to the war in Ukraine, making a new ground invasion elsewhere highly improbable over the next two months. Furthermore, no preliminary signs of troop build-ups along other borders (e.g., Baltics, Moldova, Georgia, Kazakhstan) have triggered US warnings of an impending invasion, unlike the months of intelligence released prior to the 2022 invasion of Ukraine. Without any ongoing crisis or immediate geopolitical trigger for a new invasion in such a narrow timeframe, the probability is at the minimal threshold.
